reviews.review_title: Want to love it? Change your mindset😆
reviews.review_description:
For those who bought this game and are not having any luck clearing the cutouts? Welcome to my club. I tried to play the game as it was designed and even asked help from the developers, nothing changed.
After trying different ways to play, I came up with my own way to enjoy and love the game. 
I picked the fastest electric album, the hard level, and set all songs to NO FAILING. Then I set my expectations to I am not going to get a high score because sensors just won't line me up, but it is okay because I want to use the game to challenge my shoulders mobility and reaction times. 
So for The first time today since I bought the game 3 years ago, I played over 30 minutes and sweat a lot.

A game features genuinely novel interaction mechanic which is even more fun in AR mode. Initially AR didn't worked on my Quest 3s, but now (once I've finally went through with factory resetting it) game stands out even more as a kind of distinctly embodied rhythmic playground, though now my rather small play room (~14 m^2) feels a bit cramped.  I'm not quite happy with the selection of tracks (too much 'vocals' in default tracks: my inner voice and movement tend to 'dissonate' at the speed higher than medium). The community offers a great deal custom tracks, though unfortunately unavailable in AR mode. While it's not really a dancing game (all movements share the same orientation), experience would probably benefit from better tracking (on Quest 3s my 'shadow' take all kinds of weird forms). There is clearly a place for a pose-mediated BeatSaber, maybe we need one more technological leap? I can almost sense myself playing it in AR without controllers :)

reviews.review_title: Attempts bad precedent for MRs future.
reviews.review_description: *PLEASE* DO NOT BUY THIS! Buy competitors who give us agency instead of using our spatial data against us. For my 'safety' the mixed reality mode will not let me face in the direction I know is safe in my playspace, instead enforcing its best guess on what direction *it* thinks is safer. My options are 're-center' (to one of several dangerous angles that would have me hit furniture) or switch back to VR mode (which will let me face the safe direction.). No other MR/exercise/rhythm game i've played has enforced its guesses about my space over my *knowledge* of my space. It would be terrible for MR if this was to become common. - giving these developers money for their paternalism is hurting MRs future, PLEASE DON'T. edit: More specifically, it latches on to what it sees as a wall - even in stationary mode, even if that wall is on the other side of furniture, and will ignore any playspace you mark out. Bear in mind this is a passthrough game, not an interactive one - and even interactive true MR usually does gives more freedom than this. Hell even most early access ones do.
It's really fun, and once you reach a certain difficulty level, you burn a decent amount of calories. It's also fun to play with friends as an Party Game.
All good in this app, but I would add better environments, and better music. Also, the shadow needs some work.

I think this is because it is hard when you first begin playing it to get a feel for how to play it.  At the start it can seem a little finicky, but that's just because you're bad at it! I suggest lowering accuracy and speed at the minimum to learn to feel the game. You just need to understand how the game works and build muscle memory for the moves in the tracks then it will become very intuitive and fun. I played a lot of Beat Saber till it grew old on me and needed something fresh.  This makes stretch your body in ways no other vr rhythm games does. A really great workout and with dowloadable custom tracks it is virtually infinite without paying  anything  more.

developer_response.body: Hello MaxSMM, thanks so much for your review! We will keep working to support OhShape in many ways so you can have never ending fun! :D

reviews.review_description: I had a great time with this. Surprisingly tiring but very very fun!

Odders Lab is known for making games with great concepts with big promises and delivering on none of it. this one really hurts me to admit it is bad. i wanted to like it SO MUCH. but I cannot recommend it. 1: the music SUCKS. not even slightly original or engaging. 2: it isn't meaningfully challenging as a rhythm game or a workout. and 3: THE MUSIC DOESNT SYNCH TO THE MOVES. let me be clear: you can not not not make a rhythm game where you cannot set the audio offset. I'm playing on a new quest 3, there should be no latency, but since there is, why can't I at least offset it? devs, I love the idea and the heart behind it, but the execution feels unfinished

developer_response.body: Hi jessemobile, thanks for your honest feedback! We try to cover as much as possible in our updates, but we understand our music choice is not to your liking. We honestly think the game can be a good workout on harder difficulties, specially with the Power Up sessions! And regarding the offset feature, we will study this option for future updates.

reviews.review_description:
Frustrating game for me after great moving in Best Saber and Supernatural. Needs a tutorial or has glitches because it asks me to “grab” the yellow obelisks coming towards me but doesn’t say how and I can’t find one button combo that enables that as I can in other games, so I began to get frustrated focused on that rather than form. Did it mean to say hit them?  But the “fun” is basically standing still as cutouts come towards you which is the opposite of a workout. Yes you pose with your arms and legs in differing positions then fail as you try to grab objects you can’t in between, but that’s not fun. Nor does it break a sweat. 

Visuals are bleh as well. 

You can literally stand there just trying to figure out how to grab things as the cutouts come towards you and you get the same result as if you were posing. There is no obvious change to tell you if you cleared the form or not anyway. Also I’m 5’4” and the firm coming at me in the cutout is shorter. Also odd. Do I never fit in the form cutout anyway.

developer_response.body:
Hello Texas Chef, we are sorry your first experience with OhShape didn't go well but here is a solution to your issues:

-You don't have to literaly grab the golden gems. You just have to touch them and they will fly around, don't worry, that's the intended behaviour and after doing that they will be added to your score.
-If you see the walls' height is different from your height, there must be a calibration problem. Please calibrate the floor of your Quest again (the virtual floor and the real floor should be same). It's a common issue, it isn't a big deal in games like Beat Saber or Synth Riders because it's hardly noticeable, but OhShape is quite sensitive to this.
-We are sure a perfect calibration will fix most of your issues. If you still struggle with some poses, play around with modifiers (speed, precision), practice mode and no fail mode until you get the hang of it. We assure you on higher difficulty levels you will break a sweat!

reviews.review_title: Best pre and post workout game
reviews.review_description: I use VR for Fitness mainly. I found OhShape to be the best warm up game and stretch game. I do a few songs increasing difficulty before the more demanding exercise games, and then do a couple of songs at very easy for gamified stretching, wind down. The song selection is spot on, with the added flexibility of hundreds of custom maps available from other fans of this game. It is so different from everything else, it has to be in every VR Fitness / Party Fun game collection.

I was very excited to play this game.  Unfortunately I was disappointed to find out there is no voice tutorial like in supernatural.  I needed more than the figures moving.  I couldn't catch the objects moving towards me.  I tried every way I thought of but none work.  I want to cancel the first day I used the program.  I have 14 days and I am done on the first day.  To cancel is becoming an issue because I don't see how to do it.  Can you please give me step by step instructions on how to cancel.

developer_response.body:
Hello BKLYN,

We are sorry you didn't enjoyed your first experience with OhShape. We tried to make a visual tutorial, but here is a little bit of info to solve your doubts. To catch the golden gems you just have to catch them, you don't need to grab them. If they fly around when you touch them that's the intended behaviour, they are added to your score. If you have any other doubts please reach to us at info@odderslab.com  If you still don't want the game (I understand by cancel you mean to get a refund) you have to contact Oculus support. We can't do refunds.

reviews.review_description: Cool game but, as others have mentioned, it needs positive feedback when passing through shapes. It's hard to know when you have done it right or missed it. Maybe it should make a sound or a light up  when succedsful.

developer_response.body:
Hi sktubis,

Thank you for your feedback. We will take this into account for future updates!

reviews.review_title: Company Unable or Unwilling to Fix Issue
reviews.review_description: I definitely wasted my money on this game. The body windows are so inaccurate that you're doomed to fail even on Easy Mode. I came on here to see if people are having the same issues and guess what.... they are. The developers are blaming it on a calibration issue with our headsets. Funny we each have an issue on this game but not others. The game is just not user friendly. Period. Developers are unable or unwilling to fix the issue. People who give this 3 stars or above are way too generous.

developer_response.body:
Hello JackieDark,

OhShape is more sensitive to height calibration than other games like Beat Saber or Synth Riders, that's why we always recommend people to check the calibration of their floor. That usally fixes all the problems while playing OhShape, but if you still have any issues please send us a video of you playing + the gameplay to info@odderslab.com and we will try to help you more.

reviews.review_description: We are having so much fun competing with each other. We had to buy a 2nd  face cover because we are sweating so much.

developer_response.body:
Hi sdrrt73,

Thanks for your feedback! We are happy to see OhShape is a fun, good and challenging workout for you!

reviews.review_description: I love the movement that you get but my scores never go up because my shadow freezes. I would love to be able to challenge myself with it but I can’t get my scores up because my shadow freezes and my shadow arms won’t go where my real arms go.

developer_response.body:
Hi Missy,

This issue is probably caused by a tracking problem. Playing with your hands a little bit ahead of your body improves the tracking precision on Quest, although we are already working on a software solution to fix this. Stay tuned!

reviews.review_title: I was about to give it a 2 stars
reviews.review_description:
When I watched the introductory video, I felt so sure that this game would be very interesting, but after I purchased it, played it at the first time, I regretted. Because I felt so dizzy even I just played the tutorial, I can't keep my eyes open, which is so frustrated, so I quit this game immediately.
Then I asked my boyfriend to play it. I determined if he felt the same way or he didn't like this game, I may ask for a refund. But he told he felt okay, so I decided to give it another try.
The second time when I played it, I tried an easy song, and opened the no fail mode. I concentrated on the moving block, tried to tell myself don't care about the other things, so I played a whole song.
After that I played it a few times, I thought it was fine to keep this game. It likes an indoor workout, which I enjoyed it. But it required a larger space than BEAT SABER, I don't have a large room, so I can't completely enjoy this game.

developer_response.body:
Hello!

Although OhShape requires more space than other VR games, you can activate the small room option before playing any song. This will recenter the walls so you don't need to make lateral movements!

reviews.review_description: Makes me get up and move.  Lots of fun and a great exercise workout.

A friend told me that I should try this game. I thought it would be the typical rhythm game but I'm surprised. It feels good and fun. The environment's design is cool and the colors are relaxing. I'm very satisfied and I recomend it.
I love this game much more than beat saber~ it forces me to move my arms and legs~ I got sweaty after 45 minutes of playing~ hope to get more nice music packs ~

This was one of the first VR games that I have ever tried and I thought it was great fun. Not only was it fun but I was sore the next day. It is a decent amount of movement and it's fun can't wait for more of this.
